title: phlox carolina subsp. angusta
intro: Phlox carolina subsp. angusta, also known as Carolina phlox, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its fragrant pink or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Phlox carolina subsp. angusta include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as wound healing and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the flowers in hot water to create a tea or by using the dried flowers in tinctures and infusions.
